Use Eden IoProvider on all SetupEden tests

Summary:
For `inplace=False` tests that has `setup_eden` enabled, tests are using regular FsIoProvider instead of EdenIoProvider due to `allow_eden_io` being false by default. It makes more sense to use EdenIoProvider since most of the case, `setup_eden` is meant to be testing eden related code anyways.

Not sure what the global rollout status on `allow_eden_io` is, it looks to be true by default on Mac/Windows. I see a gk https://www.internalfb.com/code/configerator-gatekeepers/[a0728edd8321eef31b0b2291eec0c8308643cda7]/source/gatekeepers/cp/cpe_buck_eden_io_everywhere.gatekeeper.cconf, but doesn't seem like it's enabled for anything?

Reviewed By: JakobDegen

Add AsyncBuildTargetResultBuilder

Summary:
This is based on receiving a series of events rather than consuming a
Stream. It still supports being used in a way that causes a future to be
cancelled for fail_fast.

fbshipit-source-id: 61776f92f11ee6c9d89dd02eedb3549c0dcaf014

add the prelude logic

Summary:
This will inject a specific list test command (when the configs are present) for generating the list test comand for android robolectric tests.

The next diff will fully test this e2e.

Notable configs:
`-c testinfra.build_codesense`

If true we will build the codesense binary (for development)
if not true we will use the dotslash version

 ---
`-c testinfra.codesense_static_listing`
If true we will inject the above RunInfo and use it in the robolectric.bzl
If not true we will not have this injected and the existing path will be used

## Rollout plan
For phase #1 we will create shadow runs for the robolectric continuous runs and inject the config `-c testinfra.codesense_static_listing` for those shadow runs. We can then compare between both runs and ensure the same tests were discovered.

After we have parity and all of the targets that won't support this (e.g., Parametrized tests)  we can move to a label based opt-out.

Reviewed By: jselbo

Differential Revision: D73158309

Include sources and resources with default manifests

Summary:
Wheels are included as directories with a the manifest generated by reading the files from the disk. https://internalfb.com/code/fbsource/fbcode/buck2/prelude/python/prebuilt_python_library.bzl?lines=76

So we need to directly read the manifests to correctly include all of the files.

Add `buck2 audit perf configured-graph-size` subcommand

Summary:
This command allows us to measure the performance of the configured
graph size calculation. This helps both to just understand the cost (so we can
    know if we should enable it more or less widely) and to more easily make
improvements.

Reviewed By: JakobDegen

Delete BuildTargetResult::collect_stream

Summary:
Use AsyncBuildTargetResultBuilder directly. Changing this makes it a lot
easier to send new events through this stream, rather than needing to construct
a stream of events and it makes refactoring it so much simpler. Also, it makes the
code look more like what we're used to seeing (i.e. just await things rather than needing
    to merge and flatten and transform streams).

Reviewed By: JakobDegen
